title: Battle of Sullust (4 ABY)

[[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] *[[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transports]] *1 [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]] *[[IG-88 assassin droid/Legends|IG-88]] *[[Gravity well projector/Legends|Gravity well projectors]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=*Minimal space forces *Moderate ground forces |casual2=*Most starfighters and bombers *Many ground forces *IG-88 damaged |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|What of the reports of the Rebel fleet massing near Sullust?|[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]|}} The '''Battle of Sullust''' was a confrontation between the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], fought during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. The battle was an attempt to lure the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] away from the [[Endor/Legends|forest moon of Endor]] in preparation for the upcoming [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. While the Rebel fleet amassed in the [[Sullust system/Legends|Sullust system]] and engaged Imperial starships, [[Renegade Squadron/Legends|Renegade Squadron]] raided the surface. Although the ground battle would eventually turn out to be a trap laid by the local Imperial officer, the squad held their own until the planet was liberated. However, [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] saw through the Alliance's plan and did not send the Imperial Navy to Sullust, as the Alliance predicted. ==Prelude== {{Quote|Nothing could be left to chance. A diversionary attack on the Imperial-controlled planet of Sullust was ordered, hoping to draw away at least some of the Imperial Fleet. We were disappointed not to be part of the Endor attack, but a lot depended on the Sullust raid. The only question was, would the Emperor take the bait…|[[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Col Serra]]|}} The Alliance to Restore the Republic planned to use the same [[Sanctuary Pipeline/Legends|hyperlane]] than the Imperials to reach [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], where the incomplete [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] was located. By that time, the [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustans]] had managed to break the pro-Imperial [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]] governmental control of Sullust and established a democratic government.{{FactFile|99|SUL 1-2|Sullust}} The planet was not able to join openly the Rebel Alliance, but the new government accepted the gathering of the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] in the system. A Rebel fleet amassed in the Sullust system where began the route. The Rebel forces then attacked the planet in order to lure the Empire. They want the Emperor to think the planet Sullust itself was the target of the Rebels, not the Death Star. As the Rebels continued to group their forces at the rallying point in the system, they launched fighters to escort the last arriving capital ships. The cruisers [[Defiance/Legends|''Defiance'']] and [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']] were due to arrive shortly. With the Imperial fleet bearing down on them, the threat to the cruisers was high. The ''Defiance'' group were already took part in small skirmishes and feared they would endure further attacks en route to Sullust. The ''Independence'' had encountered Imperial patrols as well, and they anticipated a larger force to intervene. It was critical that these cruisers to join the rendezvous point at Sullust to ensure that, under Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar's]] leadership, the attack on Endor would not be suicidal. To lure Imperial forces, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Col Serra]]'s unit, [[Renegade Squadron/Legends|Renegade Squadron]] was then deployed to capture the Imperial Garrison stationed on the planet. Prior to the battle, the unit had launched an [[Battle of Korriban (Galactic Civil War)|attack]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], where Emperor Palpatine was making a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] pilgrimage. The Emperor's shuttle contained databanks of sensitive information on his schedule, and while he was trapped in a Sith tomb by the unit, a soldier stole a databank from the shuttle, which was later used it to confirm that the Emperor would be on the Death Star II during the Alliance's attack on it. ==The battle== ===Space engagement=== {{Quote|Don't worry, we've got you covered.''"
"''Music to my ears, my boys. The Imperials aren't far behind us.''"
"''They're a little closer than you thought, Commander...|[[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace Azzameen]] and [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Devers (Rebel Alliance)|Devers]], as the [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|VSD]] ''[[Vagrant]]'' enters the system|}} [[File:Fleet_massing_near_Sullust_XWA.png|thumb|right|250px|Imperial starships approaching the [[Independence task force|''Independence'' task force]].]] Leaving the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] Cruiser [[Liberty/Legends|''Liberty'']], [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace Azzameen]] set out for the ''Defiance'' group. Upon arriving, two ''Carrack''-class cruisers and a ''Victory''-class star destroyer arrived and deployed [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] and [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]]. Eventually, the ''Defiance'' task force jumped and joined the ''Liberty''. Ace jumped back and set out for the ''Independence''. When Ace arrived in-system, he found the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Immortal/Legends|Immortal]]'', having begun their attack, with Ackbar deploying [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wings]] of [[Green Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Green Group]]. Again, Ace had to protect and escort the fleet. However, Ace was able to fend them off and allow the ''Independence'' to escape into hyperspace, a few seconds later, Ace followed. ===Ground battle=== {{Quote|We do our job here, and the Emperor will be watching at Sullust instead of seeing what's happening on Endor.|Commander Col Serra|}} [[File:Sullust_Renegade_Squadron.jpg|thumb|left|250px|[[Renegade Squadron/Legends|Renegade Squadron]] assaults the Imperial garrison on Sullust.]] After Renegade Squadron landed on the planet, they quickly stormed the Imperial outpost, which suspiciously had little to no defenses. Alarmed, Col Serra contacted Alliance command to inform them that the unit might have fallen into some sort of trap. His suspicions were correct, as an unidentified [[IG-88 assassin droid/Legends|IG-88]] unit appeared and ambushed the unit. It was equipped with a [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]], rendering it invulnerable to blaster fire. Serra thought of this as a sort of "payback" to Renegade Squadron for humiliating [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] during the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. Nevertheless, the unit quickly picked up on the source of the shield, a lightning conduit, which was quickly destroyed. The droid became vulnerable, and the unit forced it to retreat, but not before causing heavy damage to the droid. Following the IG-88's retreat and the rebel fleet jumping into hyperspace to Endor, leaving behind two MC80 Star Cruisers behind, a number of [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projectors]] were activated, preventing any escape for the Renagade Squadron. To disable the gravity wells, the shield generator would have to first be disabled. The lone Imperial officer carrying the codes was assassinated, and his codes were taken by a Renegade Squadron soldier and downloaded into the shield mainframe. Now vulnerable, the gravity well projectors were destroyed. The unit would continue to hold off Imperial reinforcements until Sullust was liberated, after which they were forced to pull out due to the imminent [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], where they were required to be present. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|We took control of Sullust, but the Emperor had seen through our plan. We were on our way to Endor before the smoke had cleared.|Commander Col Serra|}} [[File:Mc09.png|thumb|right|300px|The Rebel fleet massing near Sullust.]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] was notified of the Rebel presence in the [[Sullust system/Legends|Sullust system]] and gave these reports to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], who paid little attention to them, instead focusing on his own plot to destroy the Rebel fleet over Endor. Renegade Squadron realized they had been unsuccessful, soon heading to Endor to assist [[General/Legends|General]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] in battle. The liberation of Sullust would be the unit's [[Wiktionary:Penultimate|penultimate]] battle, before its disbanding. After the Rebel victory at Endor and the death of the Emperor, Sullust was one of the first worlds to officially join the [[Alliance of Free Planets]], and subsequently the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Don't listen to the old man when he says 'it is of no concern,' dude -- because the Renegade Squadron is plenty cause for concern.|Mark (''Battlefront: Renegade Squadron'' game programmer)|{{WebCite|url=http://blogs.ign.com/RenegadeSquadron/2007/10/02/67884|text=Sullust|work=[http://blogs.ign.com/RenegadeSquadron ''Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron'': The Official Team Blog]|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20071006035641/http://blogs.ign.com:80/RenegadeSquadron|date=2007-10-02|nolive=1}}}} "Reports of a Rebel fleet massing near Sullust" were first mentioned by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]. The gathering and subsequent space battle were featured in ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]''. The [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] never elaborated any further on the said event until ''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron]]'' was released in [[2007]], giving the details of a ground battle. ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' eventually [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]]ed that Renegade Squadron's mission was just a minor raid intended to lure Imperial forces and justify the presence of the Rebel fleet in the system. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron]]'' *[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']] *''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' {{1st}} *{{Film|VI}} {{1stm}} {{Imo}} *''[[Hard Merchandise]]'' {{Imo}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *{{FactFile|99|SUL 1-2|Sullust}} {{Imo}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{GCW_4_ABY_1}} {{Interlang |de=Schlacht von Sullust (4 NSY) }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sullust (4 ABY), battle of}} [[Category:Battles of Sullust]] [[Category:Battles of the Galactic Civil War]]
